Defendants' Motion To Compel Compliance With Independent Medical Examination And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas A. Douvan for defendant and moving party: Jay Kenyon, Scott Janecek and Green's Restaurant Everday, Inc.
Seth I. Rosenber for plaintiff and opposing party: Carole Priven

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Defendant's motion to compel compliance with the independent medical examination is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear at a time and place mutually convenient to the parties to submit to a clinical orthopedic examination. The examination may not include an "evaluation of associated structures" as that term is ambiguous and undefined by defendant. The Court cannot determine at this time whether defendant will offer Dr. Macko as an expert witness as to matters other than those within the expertise of orthopedists. Plaintiff is not precluded from moving for a protective order pursuant to CCP Section 2034.250 and Browne v. Superior Court (1979) 98 Cal. App. 3d 610 or otherwise seeking to exclude the testimony of Dr. Macko relating to accident causation, accident dynamics or biomechanics. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.

MODIFICATION: To delete portions of ruling regarding associated structures' and to clarify that the Court makes no rulings as to any potential motions pursuant to CCP 2034.250.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
